rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page, no amounts present, very legible.
Clear section title written under the year; no monetary figures on the same line.
Numeric trio '456 8 8' clearly aligned in the three currency columns; vertical rules unambiguous.
Single '10' in the pounds column; shillings/pence columns blank — interpreted as 10 pounds 0s 0d. Text legible.
Pounds digit '5' clearly present in pounds column; parentheses text '(Church Building Societ)' visible. Interpreted as £5 0s 0d.
Pounds '5' aligned under pounds column for this parenthetical line; no shillings or pence visible.
Shows '1 . 11' (appears as '1.11') with the '1' under pounds and '11' under shillings. Pence column blank. Legible but handwriting minor flourish.

rows.description: Compensation to Fellows Dry price to Michl 1859
rows.notes: Numbers '22 2 9' clearly visible and aligned. Description has some abbreviations ('Michl') — text transcribed exactly as it appears; alignment of numbers is clear.

Small '17' appears on the same horizontal level in the narrow shillings column while pounds column is blank. Column alignment is slightly ambiguous here (could be pounds), but 17 fits valid shillings (0–19) so interpreted as £0 17s 0d.

Clear trio '15 0 7' aligned in the three currency columns. There are smaller handwritten sub-lines showing individual components (e.g. 'Witham 1:12:3' and 'Bedford 13:8:4') which sum to this value; main line used.
Section label introducing several payments for the college gardener; no amount printed on the same line.
Numbers '7 4 6' are legible and aligned to the currency columns; description clearly reads 'Christmas 1859' as a sub-item of College Gardener.
Pounds '7' and shillings '13' visible. Pence column appears blank; ink and handwriting at this line are slightly fainter causing minor uncertainty about any pence.
Line reads 'Mids' (abbreviation for Midsummer); '8 5 2' aligned in currency columns but the handwriting is somewhat stylized, giving moderate ambiguity.
Final gardener sub-item reads 'Michl' (Michaelmas). Numbers '7 12 9' visible but slightly cramped; moderate confidence.
Underlined summed figure '547 5 5' clearly printed and separated by a horizontal rule. Interpreted as subtotal/total for the Soluta Ordinaria section.
Clear section heading centered on the page; no associated amount on the same line.
Right-side numbers '22 8 6' clearly visible and aligned; description reads 'Dry price to Michl: 1858' with legible handwriting.
Section label (spelled 'Guineas' as best read) with a curly brace grouping several sub-items to the right. Slight ambiguity of the exact word but group structure is clear.
Sub-item reads 'Inn Bill 3.9' — interpreted as £3 9s 0d. Alignment of '3' in pounds and '9' in shillings column is consistent with surrounding rows.
Reads 'Stamps 4.2' interpreted as £4 2s 0d; handwriting clear for these digits.
'Commrs' (abbreviation) with figures '12 18 6' clearly aligned. I read this as £12 18s 6d.
Line reads '2 Inn Bill 3.10.6' and the trio '3 10 6' is clear in the currency columns.
Second 'Stamps' entry shows a '4' aligned in pounds column; no shillings/pence visible, interpreted as £4 0s 0d.
Second 'Commrs' line with '12 12 1' legible in columns; recorded as £12 12s 1d.
Bracketed total '33 6 3' printed to the right of the Guineas subgroup; clear alignment and under a curly brace grouping the above items.
Name 'Faulkner' appears as a label for the next grouped items; text legible but slightly stylized.
Reads '45.3.10' (interpreted as £45 3s 10d). Numbers are large and clearly aligned under currency columns.
Single '40' in the pounds column next to 'Tiles'; no shillings/pence visible. Legible but note that '40' could be a larger sum and is clearly placed in pounds.
Bracketed total '85 3 10' appears to the right of the Faulkner group; digits well-formed and align with the group's columns.
Small line 'Bill & slates' with '5 9' aligned in pounds and shillings. Pence blank. Readable though somewhat squeezed between groups.
Name appears to read 'Willsden' (handwriting ambiguous between single/double 'l' and 's'); serves as a label for the following sub-items.
Reads 'Hiles Bill 3.1.8' with digits aligned; the name 'Hiles' is faint but interpretable. Currency trio fits columns.
Line shows '14.4.1/2' (handwritten '1/2' after pence). Interpreted as £14 4s and 1/2d (amount_pence_whole set to 0, amount_pence_fraction 0.5). The fractional notation is faint; moderate confidence.
Reads 'Draining 80.12' — interpreted as £80 12s 0d. This is a large number and handwriting is slightly compressed; alignment corresponds to pounds/shillings columns.

A bracketed/group total '84 8 1/2' is printed alongside the Willsden/Rowles entries. The fraction (1/2d) is visible; however the relationship between the subitems and this total is not arithmetically coherent on inspection (some group sums do not algebraically add to this), so confidence is moderate.

Line reads 'Tuckham allowed for improvement' with a '60' clearly in pounds column and a small '1' in the shillings/pence area — alignment is ambiguous between shillings and pence; interpreted here as £60 0s 1d with low–moderate confidence.
'Braine - Good. Bill' legible; numbers '3 2 5' are well-formed in the pounds/shillings/pence columns.
Label 'Gardner. Tax.' introduces a small grouped set of items; the header text is somewhat stylized but readable.
The name appears as 'Lymatte' (very uncertain reading). The adjacent figures look like '9.1' — interpreted as £9 1s 0d. Low confidence due to uncertainty in the description text.
Reads 'Basket 9.17.3' clearly in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Characters and numerals are legible.
Reads 'Watts 2.4.6' with the numeric trio clear and aligned to the currency columns.

Bracketed subtotal '12 10 10' appears next to the Gardner items. The printed total is clear, but it does not arithmetically reconcile with the three listed subitems (possible grouping/assignment ambiguity), so confidence lowered.
Section label 'Timber' (handwriting compact). Introduces several small items below.
Line reads 'Poles' with a nearby numeric '4 16' (interpreted as £4 16s 0d). The spacing between digits and columns is somewhat cramped, giving moderate uncertainty.
Reads 'Horse repair 4' — single '4' in pounds column; no shillings/pence visible. Placement is slightly ambiguous so confidence is moderate.

Line 'Pipes 6' appears with a bracket grouping that results in '8 10' printed in the currency columns near this area. Interpreted as £8 10s 0d; alignment of the small '6' in the original text is ambiguous so confidence moderate.
Reads 'Brook 2.10' next to the 'Pipes' group; numeric placement suggests £2 10s 0d. Handwriting here is small and slightly faded, moderate confidence.
Final boxed total at bottom right clearly reads '314 12 0 1/2' — interpreted as £314 12s 0d and a halfpenny (0.5). The boxed total is very legible and used as page grand total.
